old_claim.note: Leather Working provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Usable mail normally required a textile or quilted underlayer for comfort and impact cushioning; this is a soft-goods support, not a leather-work requirement.

source_shape.source_locator: The essay states that mail armor needed an underlayer of quilted fabric for comfort and cushioning, and that mail was worn on top of quilted garments.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Mail was interlinked metal-ring armor worn with textile padding rather than a leather construction technology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old leather edge conflated mail armor with leather armor or leather fittings; the source-backed soft support for wearable mail is quilted fabric.

ontology_before: Chain Mail Armor depended on iron working, bronze working, and leather working, implying leather craft was a direct support for the mail armor node.
ontology_after: Chain Mail Armor is scoped to metal-ring body armor; iron working carries the main ring-material dependency, bronze working remains a weaker adjacent fittings context, and basic weaving/basketry carries the textile underlayer support.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: chain_mail_armor now depends on basic_weaving_basketry and no longer directly depends on leather_working.
would_reject_if: The node is later narrowed to a specific leather-backed mail-and-leather composite.
A source shows the earliest scoped mail armor required leather construction rather than textile padding.
A new padded_gambeson or arming_garment node is added and should replace the broader basic_weaving_basketry dependency.
short_reason: Wearable mail is metal ring armor with textile padding support; leather working was an overbroad edge.
